faqs.question: What causes drain flies in Cortlandt homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic matter that builds up inside drains, pipes, and areas with standing water. Common causes include clogged drains, leaky pipes, seldom-used bathrooms, and moisture accumulation in basements or crawl spaces, which are common in older Cortlandt properties.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Cortlandt?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of breeding sites. Our technicians will inspect all potential problem areas, treat affected drains and pipes, and provide prevention recommendations to keep drain flies from returning.

faqs.question: Is the drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe for families and pets when applied by our licensed professionals. We can also offer eco-friendly and organic treatment options upon request. Most treatments allow you to resume normal activities within a few hours.

faqs.question: Do you offer same-day drain fly treatment in Cortlandt?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer same-day and emergency services for Cortlandt residents when our schedule permits. Drain fly infestations can worsen quickly, so we prioritize rapid response. Call us early in the day for the best chance of same-day service, with most treatments priced between $350-$550.
